warning: Formal clarity can conceal modeling choices and exclusions.
mitigation: State assumptions, domains, constraints, and omitted variables explicitly.

warning: Complex values can be reduced to narrow measurable proxies.
mitigation: Audit what the metric omits and who is affected by the reduction.

warning: A mathematically solved system can optimize the wrong objective.
mitigation: Interrogate objectives before solving and include ethical review.

warning: Proof or verification can be mistaken for full adequacy.
mitigation: Distinguish specification, proof, interpretation, and consequence.

warning: Abstraction can exclude learners or marginalized communities.
mitigation: Teach examples, history, notation, meaning, and applications alongside form.

warning: Category-level abstraction can become opaque without motivating examples.
mitigation: Connect objects and morphisms to concrete cases before generalizing.
